rulings.Case Title: BODERO & ASSOCIATES, INC. VS. JOHN GORMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 25, 2025 Line 1. DEFENDANT JOHN GORMAN DBA GORMAN CONSTRUCTION's Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs. Defendant John Gorman's unopposed motion for fees and costs on appeal is GRANTED. Defendant is entitled to $13,477.50 in fees and $1,572.88 in costs ($15,050.38). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: GENOA MARTELL VS. CONTEXTLOGIC, INC. (DBA "WISH)", A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Evidentiary Hearing On Litigation Funding And Successor Liability
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Tuesday, March 25, 2025 Line 12. PLAINTIFF GENOA MARTELL's Motion For Evidentiary Hearing On Litigation Funding And Successor Liability. Plaintiff Genoa Martell's Motion for Evidentiary Hearing On Litigation Funding And Successor Liability is DENIED. For one thing, Plaintiff's motion was not properly filed or served. Unless otherwise ordered or specifically provided by law, all moving and supporting papers shall be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing. (CCP 1005.) Where papers are filed and served electronically, a moving party must add two days for purposes of calculating the minimum filing and service periods. (CCP 1010.6(a)(3)(B).) Thus, here, Plaintiff needed to efile and serve her moving papers 16 court days plus two days before the hearing. She filed and served 16 courts days plus only one calendar day. Plaintiff is admonished to review and comply with the applicable procedural and substantive rules. For another thing, Plaintiff's motion is entirely without legal or factual support. The California Civil Discovery Act is robust and contains many tools that allow parties to develop the record and their case. (See CCP 2016 et seq.) On the relevant issues, Plaintiff has availed herself of none of these tools. Nor has she presented a record that would come anywhere close to supporting the relief requested here. Plaintiff is admonished to review and follow the Civil Discovery Act and associated rules. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
